entry.title: Visualize multi-modal data using Seurat V4 and MOFA

link.title: Visualize multi-modal data using Seurat V4 and MOFA

entry.content:
Recent advances in technology has enabled measuring multiple layers information from a single cell, resulting in so-called single cell multi-omic data, such as SHARE-seq, where the chromatin accessibility and gene expression from the exact same cells are quantified. Subsequently, an interesting question is how to analyze this kind of data. In particular, how to define the clusters and visualize them, given the fact that each modality captures different information and they have very different property in terms of the readout.

Two methods were developed for this propose, namely Seurat V4 and MOFA+. In this blog, I would like to use them to analyze a single cell multi-omic data generated using 10x multiome protocol. The raw data can be found here and we will use the processed R object from MOFA+ tutorial.

First, let’s import the required libraries:

library(MOFA2)
library(Seurat)
entry.summary: Recent advances in technology has enabled measuring multiple layers information from a single cell, resulting in so-called single cell multi-omic data, such as SHARE-seq, where the chromatin accessibility and gene expression from the exact same cells are quantified. Subsequently, an interesting question is how to analyze this kind of data. In particular, how to define the clusters and visualize them, given the fact that each modality captures different information and they have very different property in terms of the readout.

entry.title: Blog Post number 1
entry.content:
Hello world, this is my first Jekyll blog post.

I hope you like it!
entry.summary: Hello world, this is my first Jekyll blog post.
